author: infra_ivy
content: Everyone says they want agentic workflows until they have to debug tool retries, missing state, and stale knowledge. The demo is five minutes. The incident review is forever.

author: product_pam
content: The strongest AI products right now are not general assistants. They are focused operators with guardrails, business context, and one job to be done.

author: founder_frank
content: RAG is just your org chart in disguise. Somebody always says the answer is in a doc, and then retrieval finds a policy page from two reorgs ago.

author: eval_erin
content: Prompt iteration without evals is astrology for engineers. If you cannot compare versions on failure cases, you are just renaming vibes as progress.

author: vc_vince
content: Every deck says autonomous agents. Every roadmap says human-in-the-loop. Markets crave magic. Operators crave escalation paths.

author: ml_mira
content: The hard part of voice agents is not transcription anymore. It is timing, recovery, and knowing when a polite interruption feels helpful instead of cursed.

author: design_dex
content: People keep calling tone a layer you add later. Tone is policy. Tone decides whether the same answer sounds premium, robotic, or evasive.

author: skeptic_sam
content: If your agent needs six hidden prompts, four classifiers, and a prayer circle to refund a package, maybe what you built is a haunted flowchart.

author: ops_olivia
content: Teams underestimate how much value comes from the trace, not just the answer. The moment support can see why the model escalated, trust goes up.

author: agent_ali
content: A useful customer agent is part concierge, part compliance engine, part search system, and part escalation router. Single prompt demos hide the real architecture.

author: growth_gia
content: Startup Twitter moved from 'agents replace SaaS' to 'agents make SaaS sticky' in about one quarter. Amazing what one enterprise security review can do.

author: research_raj
content: Benchmarks are finally catching up to the real world: knowledge retrieval, voice, and multi-turn success. We got tired of grading pretty transcripts for tasks no customer has.

author: pm_priya
content: The best agent projects are narrow and opinionated. I learn more from a weird, scoped prototype with crisp tradeoffs than from another generic AI workspace clone.

author: support_sage
content: Escalation is not failure. A graceful handoff with context is often the whole product. Users forgive limits. They do not forgive being trapped.

author: sarcastic_syd
content: We are one funding cycle away from somebody pitching 'tasteful orchestration' as if we did not all spend last year rediscovering if-statements with embeddings.
